titlestmt.titleproper: John Martin Collection of Wyndham Lewis
titlestmt.author: by Brooke McManus

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday September 5, 2024 at 04:12

did.unittitle: John Martin collection of Wyndham Lewis

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection contains correspondence, original artwork, writings, proofs, ephemera, photographs, audiocassette tapes, and other materials by or relating to American publisher John Martin and British artists and writers Wyndham Lewis and Michael Ayrton.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Boxes 7-8 (audiocassettes): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Black Sparrow Press on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 1983.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

bioghist.head: John Martin (1930-)
bioghist.p: John Martin is an American editor, book collector, and publisher who founded Black Sparrow Press. Martin is known for his patronage of Charles Bukowski, and Black Sparrow Press published works by both avant-garde and mainstream authors, including Bukowski, Paul and Jane Bowles, John Fante, Wyndham Lewis, and Gertrude Stein.

Born in 1930, Martin was raised in Los Angeles and briefly attended the University of California, Los Angeles, before dropping out in his first year. In 1949, he began to collect first editions of American and English literature. His focus later expanded to include "new poets" and Beat poetry. Martin managed an office supply store, and he used the company's printing machine to produce early broadsides of Bukowski's poetry. Martin launched Black Sparrow Press in Los Angeles in 1966 with proceeds from the sale of first editions from his personal collection; the company's first publication was a limited run of Bukowskis poem "True Story." Martin also acquired the rights to titles by D. H. Lawrence and Wyndham Lewis, and in the 1980s, Black Sparrow Press reissued more than 20 Lewis works that had fallen into obscurity, among them the literary critique Men Without Art (1934) and Blast , a short-lived journal of the Vorticism movement. In 2002, Martin retired and sold Black Sparrow's publishing rights to several different companies including HarperCollins.

bioghist.head: Wyndham Lewis (1882-1957)
bioghist.p: Percy Wyndham Lewis was a British writer, artist, and leading figure in the Vorticist movement. He authored 40 works of fiction, nonfiction, memoir, and criticism, including the novels Tarr (1918) and The Human Age trilogy and the autobiographies Blasting and Bombardiering (1937) and Rude Assignment: A Narrative of my Career Up-to-Date (1950). Lewis joined with other London artists and poets to form the short-lived movement labeled Vorticism by Ezra Pound. Pound was also a contributor to Blast , a Vorticist literary and art journal edited and published by Lewis in 1914-1915. Although Lewis turned his focus to writing in the 1920s and 1930s, he continued to produce paintings, watercolors, and drawings, including "The Surrender of Barcelona" (1936) and portraits of Pound, Edith Sitwell, T. S. Eliot, and inventor Guglielmo Marconi. His work experienced a revival in the late 20th century when Black Sparrow Press, an independent publishing house founded by John Martin, reissued a number of Lewis's novels and essay collections.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: John Martin Collection of Wyndham Lewis. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Former call number: Uncat MS Vault 727

Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection contains correspondence, original artwork, writings, proofs, ephemera, photographs, audiocassette tapes, and other materials by or relating to American publisher John Martin and British artists and writers Wyndham Lewis and Michael Ayrton. Included are files for the National Book League exhibition and catalog Word and Image , featuring art by Lewis and Ayrton. The collection also contains Ayrton's illustrations for books by Lewis, such as The Human Age trilogy, and a small amount of material related to Black Sparrow Press reprints of Blast , the Vorticist literary magazine co-founded by Lewis. Correspondence contains letters from Lewis to Ayrton, Naomi Mitchison, and Olive Johnson, the mother of two of his children; letters from Mitchison to rare book dealer Boris Edgar-Harding; and letters to Martin from Julian Symons and others. Artwork by Lewis includes a watercolor drawing of Guglielmo Marconi.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The John Martin Collection of Wyndham Lewis is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes drafts of letters to the editor and articles such as "Mist Rising, After Winter."

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: "Blast" by D. G. Bridson / Grigson interviewed by Paul Bailey
Wyndham Lewis: "Essential Purposes of Art (1946); "When John Bull Laughs" (1938); "A Crisis of Thought" (1947)
